events.title: Lunch Poems: Cindy Juyoung Ok
events.description: A poetry reading by Cindy Juyoung Ok as part of the Lunch Poems series.

events.title: Rupture: The U.S. and the Fate of World Order (Talk 2 of 4)
events.description: The second in a four-part talk series examining the United States' role and the trajectory of world order.

events.title: Sumud Behind Bars: Palestinian Women and the Politics of Everyday Resistance
events.organizer: UC Berkeley Palestinian & Arab Studies Program
events.location: Greene & Rosenblum Studio, Grimes Engineering, UC Berkeley
events.description: A public lecture featuring Dr. Samah Saleh discussing Palestinian women's resistance.

events.title: Rebooting Tech Culture: Book Talk featuring Telle Whitney
events.organizer: UC Berkeley (in conversation with John Wawrzynek and the Berkeley Society of Women Engineers)
events.location: Greene & Rosenblum Studio, Grimes Engineering, UC Berkeley
events.description: A book talk and signing with Telle Whitney, senior executive leader, entrepreneur, and advocate for women in technology.

events.title: Startup Group Therapy (Members-only)
events.description: Startup Group Therapy (Members-only)

events.title: Luis Flores: Remaking Landed Economic Security in America | City & Regional Planning Lecture
events.location: 112 Bauer Wurster Hall, College of Environmental Design, UC Berkeley
events.description: A lecture by Luis Flores on strategies for re-establishing economic security related to land ownership in America.

events.title: Jeffrey Skoller: In Conversation & Film Presentation (Documentary Voices Series)
events.description: Jeffrey Skoller in conversation with Jonathan Mackris, presenting films on memories of war and resistance as part of the Documentary Voices series.

events.title: Problem of the Month: Framing the Problem
events.description: Problem of the Month: Framing the Problem

events.title: Film Screening: The Trip
events.description: A screening of Roger Corman's 1967 film 'The Trip,' featuring Peter Fonda and written by Jack Nicholson.

events.title: Seminar 211, Economic History: Ellen Munroe (LSE) - “Wealth and Intergenerational Political Power Mobility”
events.description: An economic history seminar featuring Ellen Munroe from the London School of Economics.

events.title: Open: Art Lab
events.description: Drop-in art making session for all ages at the Fisher Family Art Lab.

events.title: Exhibition Tour: Theresa Hak Kyung Cha: Multiple Offerings
events.description: A guided tour of the 'Theresa Hak Kyung Cha: Multiple Offerings' exhibition, led by graduate students.

events.title: Cal Baseball vs. San Diego Toreros
events.location: Evans Diamond at Stu Gordon Stadium, Berkeley, CA
events.description: Home baseball game where the Cal Bears take on the San Diego Toreros.

events.title: Women*'s History & Empowerment Month Celebration
events.organizer: UC Berkeley (in partnership with Gender Equity Resource Center)
events.description: A celebration featuring performers, activities, giveaways, and free food in honor of Women's History Month.

events.title: Sonic Socialism: Governing Crisis through Sound in Urban Vietnam
events.description: A lecture exploring how sound is used in urban Vietnam to govern during times of crisis.

events.title: ENV Seminar: Los Angeles County Sanitation Districts: A Century of Public Service
events.description: A seminar focusing on a hundred years of public service provided by the Los Angeles County Sanitation Districts.

events.title: Cultural Analytics Series: Bob L. T. Sturm “I am Troubled”
events.location: 210 South Hall and online, UC Berkeley
events.description: A presentation by Bob L. T. Sturm as part of the ongoing Cultural Analytics Series.

events.title: BETR/EECS Solid State Technologies Seminar
events.location: Hogan Room, Cory Hall (Room 521), UC Berkeley
events.description: A seminar on solid-state technologies hosted by the Berkeley Electrochemical Technologies Research (BETR) and EECS departments.

events.title: Julia Keefe Indigenous Big Band
events.description: A performance by the Julia Keefe Indigenous Big Band, presented by Cal Performances.

events.title: Film Screening: Ali: Fear Eats the Soul
events.description: A screening of Rainer Werner Fassbinder's 1974 film as part of the 'Fassbinder and the New German Cinema' series.

events.title: Cal Rugby vs. Navy
events.description: Home rugby match where the Cal Bears face off against Navy.

events.title: Cal Women's Gymnastics vs. Boise State
events.description: Home women's gymnastics meet featuring the Cal Bears against Boise State.

events.title: Cal Baseball vs. San Diego Toreros (Game 1 of DH)
events.location: Evans Diamond at Stu Gordon Stadium, Berkeley, CA
events.description: The first game of a home baseball double-header between the Cal Bears and the San Diego Toreros.

events.title: Cal Baseball vs. San Diego Toreros (Game 2 of DH)
events.location: Evans Diamond at Stu Gordon Stadium, Berkeley, CA
events.description: The second game of a home baseball double-header between the Cal Bears and the San Diego Toreros.

events.title: Charlie Chaplin's The Kid; Marc Ribot, guitar
events.description: A screening of Charlie Chaplin's 'The Kid' with live guitar accompaniment by Marc Ribot.

events.title: Conversation: Penny Cooper and Rena Rosenwasser with Margot Norton
events.description: A conversation with collectors Penny Cooper and Rena Rosenwasser, joined by Margot Norton, related to their art collection.

events.title: Cal Baseball vs. San Diego Toreros
events.location: Evans Diamond at Stu Gordon Stadium, Berkeley, CA
events.description: Home baseball game where the Cal Bears conclude their series against the San Diego Toreros.

events.title: Granulating Watercolors Workshop with Berkeley Painting Club
events.description: A hands-on workshop to learn techniques for granulating watercolors, hosted by a student organization.

events.title: The English Concert: Handel's Hercules
events.description: A performance of Handel's 'Hercules' by The English Concert, conducted by Harry Bicket.

events.title: Jitish Kallat | Artist's Talk: In Conversation with Dan Werthimer and Atreyee Gupta
events.description: An artist's talk by Jitish Kallat, engaging in a discussion with Dan Werthimer and Atreyee Gupta.

events.title: Discussion | Chile Today: A Conversation with Javiera Manzi Araneda and Claudio Alvarado Lincopi
events.description: A discussion on contemporary political and social issues in Chile with prominent speakers.

events.title: Kaffeeklatsch
events.description: An informal social gathering offering an opportunity for conversation and community building.

events.title: A Local-to-Global Approach for Decision-Making in Large Stochastic Systems (Weina Wang, Carnegie Mellon University)
events.description: A lecture by Weina Wang from Carnegie Mellon University on advanced decision-making strategies in complex systems.

events.title: Refusing To Be a Weapon: Holocaust Memory and the Jewish Left in an Age of Genocide | A Conversation with Marianne Hirsch and Benjamin Balthaser
events.description: A conversation exploring Holocaust memory and its intersection with the Jewish Left in contemporary contexts.

events.title: Lu Liang: Geospatial Intelligence for Environmental Design | Public Land, Public Space, Public Discourse
events.description: A lecture by Lu Liang on applying geospatial intelligence to environmental design and public discourse.

events.description: Startup Group Therapy (Members-only)

events.title: The Politicization of Maternal Mortality Data
events.description: A discussion examining how maternal mortality data is politicized and its implications.

events.title: What do the rich owe the rest of us?: A conversation on taxation and democracy
events.description: A conversation exploring the societal obligations of the wealthy in the context of taxation and democratic principles.

events.title: Dorothee Wierling | The German Blues. A Historian's Perspective on the Rise of Right-Wing Populism in Eastern Germany.
events.description: A historian's perspective on the emergence and growth of right-wing populism in Eastern Germany.

events.title: Sandip Roy | Chapal Rani, the Last Queen of Bengal: The Life and Times of a Female Impersonator
events.description: A talk by Sandip Roy chronicling the life and era of Chapal Rani, a renowned female impersonator.

events.title: [Panel] Careers in Labor and Social Justice
events.description: A panel discussion providing insights into various career paths within the fields of labor and social justice.

events.title: Transfer Student Social
events.location: Game Zone, Student Union, UC Berkeley
events.description: A social event offering refreshments and snacks specifically for transfer students to connect and socialize.

events.title: Seminar: “Resilient or Scarred? Migration, Mortality, and Morbidity after Natural Disasters” (Tatyana Deryugina)
events.organizer: Energy Institute at Haas, UC Berkeley
events.description: A seminar by Tatyana Deryugina from the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign on the long-term impacts of natural disasters.

events.title: Is This Your Only Life? (Foerster Lecture with Mark Johnston)

events.description: A Foerster Lecture presented by Mark Johnston, Henry Putnam University Professor from Princeton University.

events.description: A creative crafting session where students can make beaded potted plants while enjoying live music from Cub Concerts.
